rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SOROORI VS. ALDEMA INVESTMENT CORPORATION OF FLORIDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 1, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT ALDEMA INVESTMENT CORPORATION OF FLORIDA Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement. Is granted. Settlement memorialized on the record in open court. Plaintiff stated that he agreed to dismiss the case as part of the settlement, and that the ageement to the terms was not under duress or pressure, and was based on his own volition. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PLATINUM INVESTMENT CORPORATION VS. STEPHEN GUDE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 1, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT STEPHEN GUDE MOTION TO TAX COSTS. Motion denied, however, attorney fee award shall be plaintiff's agreed reduced amount of $12,806.38. Billing rate is reasonable and work performed reasonable and necessary. Costs supported by Heim declaration and time sheets submitted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HOSUN YOO VS. JOHN EVAN HEMINGWAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 1, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFF HOSUN YOO Motion for Entry of Judgment. Motion denied. The 3/3/2010 letter was not an absolute and unqualified acceptance of defendant's offer. Bias vs. Wright (2002) 103 Cal.App. 4th 811. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TARCISIO VEGA VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 1, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is granted, without leave to amend, no opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TARCISIO VEGA VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 1, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is sustained, with one final leave to amend granted. Plaintiff must allege facts supporting allegations of failure to provide meal and rest breaks (3rd cause of action.) Plaintiff must comply with CCP section 382 to bring a representative action under B&P section 17200. Absent class certification, relief and restitution cannot extend beyond the named plaintiff. Harper vs. 24 Hour Fitness, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App. 4th 966. (5th cause of action.)=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CARMEN CORTEZ VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 1, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer moot as to 1st cause of action, failure to pay regular wages in view of court's ruling on motion to strike. Sustained without leave to amend as to 3rd cause of action (failure to provide rest and meal breaks) because court previously granted motion for judgment on the pleadings without leave to amend. Sustained with one final leave to amend as to 5th cause of action (B&P sec. 17200). Plaintiff must comply with CCP section 382 to bring representative action under B&P sec. 17200. Absent classs certification, relief and restitution cannot extend beyond the named plaintiff. Harper vs. 24 Hour Fitness, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App. 4th 966.=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CARMEN CORTEZ VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 1, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is granted without leave to amend, no opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ERICK PORRAS VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 1, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is sustained, with one final leave to amend granted. Plaintiff must allege facts supporting allegations of failure to pay regular wages (1st cause of action). Plaintiff must allege facts supporting allegations of failure to provide meal and rest break periods (3rd cause of action.) Plaintiff must comply with CCP section 382 to bring a representative action under B&P sec. 17200. Absent class certification, relief and restitution cannot extend beyond the named plaintiff. Harper vs. 24 Hour Fitness Inc. 167 Cal.App. 4th 966. (5th cause of action).=(302/CWW)
